task:
The handling of trees via go-git seem to not be working effectively and erroring early, as not all objects are present. This is the current error:

2026/03/13 05:53:57 WARN FetchingTree.ensureBlobs: tree walk failed tree_hash=c95a503a622038c7564f41ad265e5c54add77e89 error="object not found"
2026/03/13 05:53:57 WARN ReadCheckpointMetadataFromSubtree failed, checking remote component=resume checkpoint_id=cab75de47439 subtree_hash=c95a503a622038c7564f41ad265e5c54add77e89 error="failed to find metadata.json in checkpoint subtree: file not found"
2026/03/13 05:53:57 WARN FetchingTree.ensureBlobs: tree walk failed tree_hash=c95a503a622038c7564f41ad265e5c54add77e89 error="object not found"
Checkpoint 'cab75de47439' found in commit but its metadata could not be read from entire/checkpoints/v1.
The metadata branch exists but checkpoint content may be missing or unreadable (e.g. after a partial fetch).
Try a full fetch:
  git fetch origin entire/checkpoints/v1:entire/checkpoints/v1

Investigate the issue. Would using TreeEntryFile instead of the current approach resolve the issue?
